sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.124 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: A group$G$is said to be if$G$has a finite-index subgroup isomorphic to the fundamental group of a special complex (in the sense of F.\,Haglund, D.\,T.\,Wise, , 17, no.\,5 (2008), 1551--1620; cf 20.60.) A group$G$is called a if it acts properly discontinuously and cocompactly by isometries on a CAT(0) metric space. [r]{a)} Is every CAT(0) free-by-cyclic group virtually special?
